source.title: Impaired Hippocampal-Cortical Interactions during Sleep in a Mouse Model of Alzheimer's Disease.

confidence_rationale: Supplies the mechanistic substrate for MECH-172's central premise -- that the consolidation pipeline (specifically SWR-DW and spindle-DW coupling) is broken in AD. This is the broken-pipeline limb of MECH-172's architectural argument. Source quality is high (Curr Biol, multi-electrode array, McNaughton lab); mapping fidelity is good but the model is 3xTg mouse (amyloid + tau), not human early AD. Transfer risk is moderate-to-substantial.
failure_signatures: If MECH-172's premise that AD specifically degrades the offline consolidation pipeline were wrong, AD mice should show normal SWR-DW coupling (they did not) and behavioral spatial learning would not correlate with that coupling (it does).

mapping.source_claim_statement: 3xTg-AD mice show impaired hippocampal sharp-wave-ripple (SWR) to cortical delta-wave (DW) coupling and impaired spindle-DW coupling during slow-wave sleep, despite increased total sleep. SWR-DW coupling correlates with next-day spatial-reorientation performance in controls, and this correlation is lost in AD mice.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-172 asserts that AD breaks the contextual attribution system (E3 hippocampal) by degrading the offline consolidation pipeline. Benthem provides the direct electrophysiological substrate: the SWR-DW and spindle-DW coupling that REE identifies with hippocampus-to-cortex schema installation is exactly what is broken. The procedural/habit system, which does not require this pipeline, would be expected to remain functional under the same pathology.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The model is 3xTg mouse (familial-AD-like amyloid + tau under transgenic promoters), not sporadic human AD. The temporal trajectory of pathology in 3xTg differs from human AD. The behavioural test is spatial reorientation, not procedural memory directly -- so this paper supports the broken-consolidation premise but does not by itself test the preservation-of-habit prediction.
mapping.source_context: 3xTg-AD mice vs C57 controls; chronic recording arrays in isocortex and hippocampus; spatial reorientation task; rest-task-rest sessions.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality electrophysiology; the mouse-model transfer is the main limitation. The within-paper correlation between SWR-DW coupling and behavioural performance strengthens the causal reading.
